intent: Fix audiobook player cover letterbox regression by adding shrink-0 to the square cover container, defensively safelisting Tailwind v4 utilities via @source inline(), and adding an E2E regression test with a non-square fixture asserting the img rect equals the container rect.

findings.title: @source inline() safelist hides root cause and accumulates dead weight
findings.summary: The defensive safelist for aspect-square, object-cover, h-full, w-full, rounded-[24px], shrink-0, max-w-80, overflow-hidden, bg-muted is paired with a comment claiming Tailwind v4's source scanner 'silently drops' these. None of these utilities are used dynamically — they are all literal class strings in AudiobookRenderer.tsx and would be picked up by @source '../**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}'. If a regression ever occurred, the real cause was almost certainly something else (e.g., an HMR/build cache or a stale dev server, see Worktree E2E warning in CLAUDE.md). Keeping the safelist as a permanent fixture papers over the unknown and ships extra bytes in every build.
findings.suggestedFix: Either (a) reproduce the original regression deterministically and document the actual Tailwind v4 cause in a docs/solutions/ note, then keep only the specific class that needed safelisting; or (b) drop the @source inline() entirely now that the regression test exists — the E2E spec is the durable safety net, the safelist is not.

findings.title: shrink-0 is the load-bearing fix but is not called out in the comment
findings.summary: The added `shrink-0` class on the cover frame is the actual behavioral change (preventing the flex parent at line 463 from shrinking the square frame and letting the img reveal bg-muted bars). The block comment above the div explains the testids and the safelist motivation but does not mention shrink-0. A future reader doing 'git blame' on this line will see a multi-purpose change without knowing which class fixes which symptom. Without that context the safelist is the obvious 'fix' and shrink-0 looks incidental — exactly backwards.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a one-line note in the comment: 'shrink-0 prevents the parent flex column from shrinking the aspect-square frame on short viewports, which was the root cause of the letterbox.' Or move shrink-0 to its own line with an end-of-line comment.

findings.title: Inline-duplicated mockAudioElement instead of shared helper
findings.summary: The spec inlines mockAudioElement with a comment saying 'Inline-duplicated (not imported) to keep this regression spec independently runnable.' Independence is a value, but the project already has shared support helpers (tests/support/helpers/seed-helpers.ts is imported here), so the file is not standalone in any meaningful sense. Inline duplication of a non-trivial HTMLMediaElement prototype mock means future fixes (e.g., to handle pause/seek) need to be applied in two places, and one will silently drift. The streaming spec referenced in the JSDoc is the original; the duplication will rot.
findings.suggestedFix: Extract mockAudioElement into tests/support/helpers/audio-mock.ts (or similar) and import from both streaming.spec.ts and this spec. The 'independently runnable' goal is preserved — the helper lives under tests/support/, which every spec already depends on.

findings.title: Aspect-ratio computed-style assertion may flake across browsers
findings.summary: expect(measurements.containerComputed.aspectRatio).toBe('1 / 1') asserts the exact serialized form. Some Chromium/Firefox/Safari versions normalize aspect-ratio differently ('1', '1 / 1', 'auto 1 / 1'). If the project ever runs this spec in non-Chromium projects (test:e2e currently runs Chromium per CLAUDE.md, but Playwright config can change), the test will fail for a styling reason that has nothing to do with letterbox. The geometric assertion (Math.abs(width - height) < 0.5) already covers 'is square' — the computed-style assertion is redundant.
findings.suggestedFix: Drop the aspectRatio === '1 / 1' check, or relax to /^1(\s*\/\s*1)?$/. The width-equals-height geometric check is the meaningful assertion.

findings.title: Plan Phase 3 verification scope is partially covered
findings.summary: Plan Phase 3 explicitly lists 'Locations to check: main player, mini-player thumbnail (AudioMiniPlayer.tsx), Library card grid, blurred backdrop at AudiobookRenderer.tsx:398.' The fix and the regression test only cover the main player. AudioMiniPlayer.tsx already uses flex-shrink-0 + fixed w-14 h-14 (so the same shrink-0 root cause does not apply there), and the blurred backdrop uses background-image: cover (different mechanism), so the fix is correctly scoped. But the plan asked for a verification pass at the other two locations; the diff and spec do not document that those were verified and intentionally not regressed. Risk is low (different rendering paths) but the plan's explicit ask is unaddressed in writing.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a one-line note to the spec's JSDoc or a brief comment in the Phase-3 section of the plan stating that AudioMiniPlayer (fixed-size flex item) and the blurred backdrop (background-image) use different rendering paths and were not affected. Optional: extend the spec to also assert the mini-player cover rect equals its 56x56 container.

findings.title: Safelist enumerates classes already statically present in JSX
findings.summary: Every class in the @source inline() list (aspect-square, shrink-0, object-cover, h-full, w-full, rounded-[24px], max-w-80, overflow-hidden, bg-muted) appears literally in AudiobookRenderer.tsx and is therefore already discovered by the @source '../**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}' scan. The safelist is a no-op under normal Tailwind v4 behavior. If it ever becomes load-bearing, the project has a different bug — and the comment will mislead the next person debugging it.
findings.suggestedFix: Either delete the safelist (E2E test catches regressions) or narrow it to a single class with a documented past failure. Do not keep all nine classes 'just in case.'

residual_risks: Regression test depends on Tailwind v4 source-scan picking up classes from a static JSX file; the very mechanism the safelist purports to defend against would invalidate the test's premise.
If a future ABS server returns covers wider than tall (landscape), object-cover crops the top/bottom — visual parity with the iOS app needs spot-check for landscape sources, not just portrait.
testing_gaps: No assertion that the blurred background at AudiobookRenderer.tsx:449-461 still renders correctly with the new shrink-0 sibling.
No assertion that the mini-player cover (AudioMiniPlayer.tsx) still fills its 56x56 frame — separate code path but same plan section.
verdict: Ready with fixes (LOW priority — none block merge)
